title: Some customer phone numbers are wrong in TaskFlow
body: We noticed that some of our customer contact records have incorrect phone numbers. The numbers look truncated or mangled. Our support team caught it because they tried calling a few customers and got wrong numbers. We have about 2,000 customer records and I'd estimate maybe 50-100 have wrong numbers. This is a serious data integrity issue for us.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — data integrity issues like this are definitely worth tracking down.

How did these customer phone numbers get into TaskFlow originally?

- **Manual entry** through the TaskFlow UI
- **Bulk import** (e.g., CSV upload or migration from another system)
- **API integration** syncing from another tool (CRM, billing system, etc.)
- **A mix** of the above
- **Something else**

This will help us figure out where the corruption is happening.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: They come in through an API integration — we have a nightly sync job that pulls customer data from our Salesforce CRM into TaskFlow. It runs automatically every night. I think the numbers were fine up until maybe two weeks ago? That's roughly when our support team started noticing the bad numbers, though it took a few days before they flagged it to me.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really helpful, thanks. The next thing that would narrow this down significantly: when you look at those ~50-100 bad numbers, are they correct in Salesforce but wrong in TaskFlow, or are they wrong in both places?

- **Correct in Salesforce, wrong in TaskFlow** — points to a bug in the sync job
- **Wrong in both systems** — the problem is upstream of TaskFlow
- **Not sure yet** — haven't had a chance to compare

If you happen to have an example of what a mangled number looks like (e.g., digits missing, extra characters, wrong format), that would also be useful, but no pressure if you don't have one handy.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: They're correct in Salesforce but wrong in TaskFlow, so it's definitely something in the sync. I pulled up a few examples to compare — it looks like the country code part of international numbers is getting cut short. Like one customer in Ireland should be +353 but in TaskFlow it's showing as +35. Another one that should start with +1268 is just showing +12. Weirdly, our US and UK customers seem fine — their numbers with +1 and +44 look correct. So it seems like it's only affecting certain international numbers.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Salesforce sync truncates international phone country codes longer than 2 digits","problem":"The nightly Salesforce-to-TaskFlow customer sync job is truncating the country code portion of international phone numbers to 2 digits. Numbers with 1-2 digit country codes (+1 US, +44 UK) are unaffected, but longer country codes are mangled (e.g., +353 Ireland → +35, +1268 → +12). Approximately 50-100 of ~2,000 customer records are affected. The issue started roughly two weeks ago.","root_cause_hypothesis":"A recent change (within the last ~2 weeks) to the sync job's phone number parsing or storage logic is truncating the country code field to 2 characters. This could be a schema change (e.g., country_code column width reduced), a regex/parsing change that extracts only the first 2 digits after '+', or a new formatting/validation step that incorrectly limits country code length.","reproduction_steps":["Identify a customer record in Salesforce with a country code longer than 2 digits (e.g., an Irish number starting with +353)","Wait for the nightly sync to run (or trigger it manually)","Compare the phone number in TaskFlow to the Salesforce source","Observe that the country code is truncated to 2 digits in TaskFlow"],"environment":"Nightly automated sync job pulling customer data from Salesforce CRM into TaskFlow via API integration","severity":"high","impact":"Support team cannot reach ~50-100 international customers by phone. Every nightly sync run likely re-corrupts any manually corrected numbers. Data integrity issue that erodes trust in the system.","recommended_fix":"Review the sync job's commit/deploy history from the past 2-3 weeks for changes to phone number handling. Check for: (1) database schema changes to phone or country_code fields, (2) changes to phone number parsing logic (regex, string slicing, or library updates), (3) new validation or formatting steps. Fix the truncation, then run a one-time re-sync or correction job to repair the ~50-100 affected records from Salesforce source data.","proposed_test_case":"Sync a test customer record with a 3+ digit country code (e.g., +353 1 234 5678 for Ireland) and verify the full number including complete country code is stored correctly in TaskFlow. Include edge cases: +1 (1 digit), +44 (2 digits), +353 (3 digits), +1684 (4 digits).","information_gaps":["Exact date of the last known good sync (reporter estimated ~2 weeks ago)","Whether a specific deployment or configuration change coincided with the onset"]}
